commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Then he returned, and walked in the house to and fro; and went up, and stretched himself upon him: and the child sneezed seven times, and the child opened his eyes. Septuagint, "he breathed "(Calmet) Other copies, (Alexandrian and Vatican) "he bent down upon the child seven times, and the child opened his eyes. Protestants, "sneezed "(Haydock) in which interpretation, Junius, Montanus, agree. Arabic, "he turned his eyes about seven times. "Others, "he trembled "(Calmet) or sighed; (ver. ) and sneezing is accounted good for alleviating the pain. Sternutamenta capitis gravedinem emend ant. (Pliny, xxviii.
